title: Move database configuration to environment variables
body:
## Description
Database credentials are currently hardcoded in the source code. This is a security risk and makes it difficult to deploy to different environments.

## Location
- **File**: `src/database.js`
- **Line**: 5

## Current Implementation
```javascript
const pool = new Pool({
    host: 'localhost',
    port: 5432,
    database: 'myapp',
    user: 'admin',
    password: 'password123'
});
```

## Proposed Solution
- Use environment variables for all database configuration
- Add `.env.example` file with required variables
- Update documentation with setup instructions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Database config uses environment variables
- [ ] No hardcoded credentials in source code
- [ ] Documentation updated

title: Add query logging for debugging
body:
## Description
Implement query logging to help with debugging database operations and performance monitoring.

## Location
- **File**: `src/database.js`
- **Function**: `executeQuery()`
- **Line**: 19

## Proposed Solution
- Log SQL queries with parameters
- Include execution time
- Add log levels (debug, info, error)
- Consider using a logging library

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Queries are logged with parameters
- [ ] Execution time is tracked
- [ ] Logging can be enabled/disabled via config

title: Implement connection retry logic
body:
## Description
Add retry logic for database connections to handle transient failures and improve reliability.

## Location
- **File**: `src/database.js`
- **Function**: `executeQuery()`
- **Line**: 20

## Proposed Solution
- Implement exponential backoff retry strategy
- Configure max retry attempts
- Add timeout handling
- Log retry attempts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Failed queries are retried with backoff
- [ ] Max retry attempts are configurable
- [ ] Retry attempts are logged

title: Add caching for frequently accessed users
body:
## Description
Implement caching mechanism to reduce database load for frequently accessed user data.

## Location
- **File**: `src/database.js`
- **Function**: `getUserById()`
- **Line**: 36

## Proposed Solution
- Use Redis or in-memory cache
- Implement cache invalidation strategy
- Add TTL for cached entries
- Monitor cache hit/miss rates

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] User data is cached after first fetch
- [ ] Cache invalidation works correctly
- [ ] Performance improvement is measurable

title: Add input validation for user creation
body:
## Description
Implement proper input validation to prevent invalid data from being stored in the database.

## Location
- **File**: `src/database.js`
- **Function**: `createUser()`
- **Line**: 48

## Proposed Solution
- Validate username format and length
- Validate email format
- Check for required fields
- Sanitize inputs
- Return meaningful error messages

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All user inputs are validated
- [ ] Invalid inputs return clear error messages
- [ ] SQL injection is prevented

title: Hash password before storing
body:
## Description
Passwords are currently stored in plain text. This is a critical security vulnerability.

## Location
- **File**: `src/database.js`
- **Function**: `createUser()`
- **Line**: 49

## Proposed Solution
- Use bcrypt or argon2 for password hashing
- Add salt to passwords
- Implement password strength validation
- Update login logic to compare hashed passwords

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Passwords are hashed before storage
- [ ] Password verification works correctly
- [ ] No plain text passwords in database

title: Implement transaction support
body:
## Description
Add support for database transactions to ensure data consistency for multi-step operations.

## Location
- **File**: `src/database.js`
- **Line**: 62

## Proposed Solution
- Create transaction wrapper functions
- Implement commit/rollback logic
- Add transaction isolation levels
- Handle nested transactions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Transaction API is available
- [ ] Rollback works on errors
- [ ] Nested transactions are handled

title: Add database migration system
body:
## Description
Implement a migration system to manage database schema changes across environments.

## Location
- **File**: `src/database.js`
- **Line**: 63

## Proposed Solution
- Use a migration tool (e.g., node-pg-migrate, Knex)
- Create initial migration for current schema
- Add migration scripts to version control
- Document migration workflow

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Migration system is set up
- [ ] Migrations can be run up/down
- [ ] Migration history is tracked

title: Implement connection pooling optimization
body:
## Description
Optimize database connection pooling configuration for better performance and resource usage.

## Location
- **File**: `src/database.js`
- **Line**: 64

## Proposed Solution
- Configure pool size based on load testing
- Add connection timeout settings
- Implement connection health checks
- Monitor pool metrics

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pool configuration is optimized
- [ ] Connection metrics are monitored
- [ ] Performance is improved

title: Improve email validation regex
body:
## Description
Current email validation regex is too simple and may allow invalid emails or reject valid ones.

## Location
- **File**: `src/utils.js`
- **Function**: `validateEmail()`
- **Line**: 17

## Current Implementation
```javascript
const regex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
```

## Proposed Solution
- Use a more comprehensive email validation regex
- Consider using a validation library (e.g., validator.js)
- Add tests for edge cases

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Valid emails are accepted
- [ ] Invalid emails are rejected
- [ ] Edge cases are handled

title: Use UUID library instead of Math.random
body:
## Description
Current ID generation using Math.random() is not cryptographically secure and may produce collisions.

## Location
- **File**: `src/utils.js`
- **Function**: `generateId()`
- **Line**: 27

## Current Implementation
```javascript
return Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9);
```

## Proposed Solution
- Use uuid library (v4 for random UUIDs)
- Or use crypto.randomUUID() for Node.js 14.17+
- Update tests accordingly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] IDs are generated using UUID
- [ ] No collisions in testing
- [ ] Performance is acceptable

title: Implement proper XSS protection
body:
## Description
Current input sanitization only trims whitespace, leaving the application vulnerable to XSS attacks.

## Location
- **File**: `src/utils.js`
- **Function**: `sanitizeInput()`
- **Line**: 37

## Current Implementation
```javascript
return input.trim();
```

## Proposed Solution
- Use a sanitization library (e.g., DOMPurify, xss)
- Escape HTML special characters
- Implement context-aware sanitization
- Add Content Security Policy headers

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] XSS attacks are prevented
- [ ] Legitimate content is preserved
- [ ] Security tests pass

title: Add validation for negative numbers in pagination
body:
## Description
Pagination calculation doesn't validate for negative numbers, which could cause unexpected behavior.

## Location
- **File**: `src/utils.js`
- **Function**: `calculatePagination()`
- **Line**: 48

## Proposed Solution
- Validate that page and limit are positive integers
- Set minimum and maximum values
- Return error for invalid inputs
- Add default values

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Negative numbers are rejected
- [ ] Zero values are handled
- [ ] Reasonable limits are enforced

title: Add caching utilities

title: Add graceful shutdown handling
body:
## Description
Implement graceful shutdown to properly close connections and finish pending requests.

## Location
- **File**: `src/app.js`
- **Line**: 33

## Proposed Solution
- Listen for SIGTERM and SIGINT signals
- Stop accepting new requests
- Wait for pending requests to complete
- Close database connections
- Exit cleanly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Server shuts down gracefully
- [ ] Pending requests complete
- [ ] Connections are closed properly

title: Implement database connection pooling in app.js
body:
## Description
Ensure database connection pooling is properly initialized and managed in the main application file.

## Location
- **File**: `src/app.js`
- **Line**: 34

## Proposed Solution
- Initialize database pool on startup
- Handle connection errors
- Close pool on shutdown
- Monitor pool health

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pool is initialized on startup
- [ ] Pool is closed on shutdown
- [ ] Connection errors are handled
